concepts.name: Node
concepts.short_definition: A fundamental building block of a linked list, containing data and a pointer to the next node.

concepts.name: Data Field
concepts.short_definition: The part of a node that stores the actual information.

concepts.name: Next Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer within a node that refers to the subsequent node in the sequence.

concepts.name: Head Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer that indicates the first node in the linked list.

concepts.name: Tail Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer that indicates the last node in the linked list.

concepts.name: Null Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A special pointer value indicating the end of the list or an empty list.

concepts.name: Singly Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure where each element (node) points to the next element.

concepts.name: Traversal
concepts.short_definition: The process of visiting each node in a linked list sequentially.

concepts.name: Insertion
concepts.short_definition: The operation of adding a new node to the linked list.

concepts.name: Insertion at Head
concepts.short_definition: Adding a new node at the beginning of the linked list.

concepts.name: Insertion at Tail
concepts.short_definition: Adding a new node at the end of the linked list.

concepts.name: Insertion in Middle
concepts.short_definition: Adding a new node at a specific position within the linked list.

concepts.name: Deletion
concepts.short_definition: The operation of removing a node from the linked list.

concepts.name: Deletion of Head
concepts.short_definition: Removing the first node from the linked list.

concepts.name: Deletion of Tail
concepts.short_definition: Removing the last node from the linked list.

concepts.name: Deletion in Middle
concepts.short_definition: Removing a node at a specific position or with a specific value.

concepts.name: Search
concepts.short_definition: The operation of locating a node with a specific value in the linked list.

concepts.name: Empty List
concepts.short_definition: A linked list that contains no nodes.

concepts.name: Single Node List
concepts.short_definition: A linked list containing only one node.

concepts.name: Dynamic Size
concepts.short_definition: The ability of a linked list to grow or shrink in size as needed.

concepts.name: Memory Allocation
concepts.short_definition: Nodes are allocated memory dynamically during runtime.

concepts.name: Sequential Access
concepts.short_definition: Elements in a linked list can only be accessed by traversing from the beginning.

concepts.name: Pointer Manipulation
concepts.short_definition: The core operations involve updating and managing the 'next' pointers.

concepts.name: Reversing a List
concepts.short_definition: Changing the order of nodes in a linked list so the last becomes the first.

concepts.name: Cycle Detection
concepts.short_definition: Identifying if a linked list contains a cycle (a node points back to a previous node).

concepts.name: Floyd's Cycle-Finding Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm to detect cycles in a linked list using two pointers moving at different speeds.

concepts.name: Finding Middle Element
concepts.short_definition: Locating the node at the middle position of a linked list.

concepts.name: Two Pointers
concepts.short_definition: Using two pointers to traverse the list simultaneously for specific operations.

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: The amount of memory required by a linked list, typically O(n) for storage.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: The time taken for operations like traversal, insertion, and deletion, often O(n).

concepts.name: Advantages of Linked Lists
concepts.short_definition: Benefits of using linked lists, such as dynamic size and efficient insertions/deletions.

concepts.name: Disadvantages of Linked Lists
concepts.short_definition: Drawbacks of using linked lists, such as no random access and overhead of pointers.

concepts.name: Linked List Implementation
concepts.short_definition: How to implement a singly linked list using programming language constructs.

concepts.name: Recursive Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Traversing a linked list using recursive function calls.

concepts.name: Iterative Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Traversing a linked list using loops.

concepts.name: Node Swapping
concepts.short_definition: Exchanging the positions of two nodes within the linked list.

concepts.name: Sorted Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linked list where the nodes are arranged in a specific order.

concepts.name: Merging Linked Lists
concepts.short_definition: Combining two or more linked lists into a single list.

concepts.name: Linked List as an Abstract Data Type
concepts.short_definition: Viewing a linked list as a conceptual data structure with defined operations.
